claim: Skills are a progressive disclosure pattern: only YAML frontmatter is loaded in system prompt; agent reads full skill.md on demand
actionable: Structure skills with brief YAML frontmatter that signals when to use a skill and longer implementation details in markdown body
source_title: Using skills with Deep Agents CLI

claim: Small number of atomic tools (shell, file manipulation) plus skills directory significantly expands action space without bloating function calling
actionable: Design agents with 5-20 core tools and a skills directory instead of binding 50+ tool functions; reduces token overhead and cognitive load
source_title: Using skills with Deep Agents CLI

claim: Bloating tool calling layer with 20+ functions causes confusion; agent can't decide which to call; similar tools get mixed up
actionable: Limit tool definitions to essential atomic operations; express higher-level workflows as skills with scripts, not as tool functions
source_title: Using skills with Deep Agents CLI

claim: Agent file system access is critical dependency for skills to work; agent must read skill.md and execute scripts
actionable: Ensure agents have file system access (local or sandboxed) before implementing skills; it's not optional
source_title: Using skills with Deep Agents CLI

claim: Deep Agents CLI supports global and project-level skills; global skills reuse across projects, project skills are scoped
actionable: Organize shared skills globally and project-specific skills locally for maximum reusability
source_title: Using skills with Deep Agents CLI
